notes: Assertion 'codebase-explored' passes 100% in both configurations — both with and without skill explored the codebase well. Not a differentiator.
Assertion 'go-nogo-verdict' passes 100% in both configurations — both produce clear recommendations. Not a differentiator.

Assertion 'scope-altitude' (no functional requirements) is the strongest differentiator: 100% with_skill vs 0% without_skill. Without the skill, both runs produced full schema designs and implementation details.
Assertion 'template-followed' failed once with_skill due to missing 'investigate' section on clear-cut Go verdict — potentially a false failure. Consider making conditional.
With-skill runs take ~45% longer (255s vs 176s) and use ~15% more tokens (51.7k vs 45k). The time increase correlates with deeper interviews (14-16 questions vs 9-10).
Without-skill runs both scored exactly 2/7, failing on the same 5 assertions. Failure mode is consistent: scope creep into design territory.
Graders consistently suggest adding assertions for: interview question altitude, AskUserQuestion usage, codebase-before-questions ordering, and scope-to-transcript traceability.
